meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. An intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship high-quality apps.
content.answer_block: Overcoming a lack of portfolio requires high-velocity shipping. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative Git-based development, you can generate production-grade micro-SaaS projects in hours. This workflow transforms abstract ideas into version-controlled, professional case studies that showcase technical depth and consistent deployment patterns.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the freelancer's greatest adversary. You have the skills, but the client needs proof—a tangible trail of commits, clean architecture, and deployed endpoints that speak louder than a resume. Without a portfolio, you are fighting for scraps in a crowded market, unable to demonstrate the speed or precision that high-paying clients demand.

But the game has changed. By integrating AI-driven scaffolding with intelligent Git workflows, you can stop 'practicing' and start 'shipping.' This is not about generating code; it is about engineering a professional narrative through version control, turning every AI-assisted session into a verifiable piece of evidence for your engineering expertise.

steps.title: Architecting the Proof of Concept
steps.description: Define a high-value micro-SaaS feature that solves a common business pain point to serve as your portfolio cornerstone.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular directory structure for a Next.js SaaS boilerplate including authentication, Stripe integration, and a PostgreSQL schema. Focus on clean code principles and scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --setup 'initialize project with recommended folder structure and dependencies'

steps.title: Establishing the Git Narrative
steps.description: Initialize your repository with a structured commit history that demonstrates incremental, logical development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a comprehensive .gitignore and a set of Conventional Commits standards for this project to ensure a professional commit history.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && claude commit 'feat: initialize project scaffolding with modular architecture'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Development
steps.description: Build the core functionality using AI to ensure the code is production-ready and documented.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust API route for user authentication using NextAuth. Include error handling, logging, and type definitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement secure auth routes with unit tests'

steps.title: Documentation as Evidence
steps.description: Generate a professional README that explains the 'Why' behind your architectural decisions, not just the 'How'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md that includes an architecture diagram description, tech stack justification, and installation instructions for a portfolio visitor.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate documentation for current codebase features'

steps.title: Deploying the Proof
steps.description: Push your work to a live environment, creating a URL that you can attach to your proposals.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for deployment preparation, including environment variable management and build optimization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'verify build readiness and generate deployment configuration'

pro_tips: Always use atomic commits; one feature per commit makes your GitHub activity graph look deliberate and highly skilled.
Include a 'Technical Decisions' document in your repo; clients love seeing how you navigated trade-offs.
Use Claude Code to write tests for your core logic; nothing signals 'Senior Developer' faster than a green test suite.
Embed your live project link directly into your GitHub profile bio to ensure immediate visibility for recruiters.
Refactor your AI-generated code once manually; the slight human touch ensures you understand the codebase perfectly for client interviews.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, replicate this process for two different tech stacks to show versatility. Then, create a 'Portfolio Hub' repository that links all your individual project repos together with a unified README.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect client to find you—build the evidence they need to hire you today. By mastering the synergy between AI and version control, you turn every hour spent coding into a permanent asset. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
